ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transportation_data" (
  "geocode" TEXT /* Unique geocode identifier for the data point */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of data collection */,
  "zone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the zone where the data was collected */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of data collection */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of any special event affecting transportation usage */,
  "bicycle" INTEGER /* Number of bicycles used */,
  "bus" INTEGER /* Number of bus rides taken */,
  "car_van" INTEGER /* Number of car or van trips */,
  "foot" INTEGER /* Number of trips made on foot */,
  "home" INTEGER /* Number of trips from home */,
  "moto" INTEGER /* Number of motorcycle trips */,
  "other" INTEGER /* Number of other transportation modes used */,
  "taxi" INTEGER /* Number of taxi rides taken */,
  "train_metro" INTEGER /* Number of train or metro rides */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("geocode")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "geocode" TEXT /* Unique geocode identifier for the location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "zone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the zone where the location is situated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("geocode")
)

CREATE TABLE "transport_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transportation mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the transportation mode (e.g., Bicycle, Bus) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transportation mode */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the mode is public transportation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, planner) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "geocode" TEXT /* Geocode of the transportation data accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_geocode FOREIGN KEY ("geocode") REFERENCES transportation_data ("geocode")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the generated report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., monthly, annual) */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analysis method used to generate the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES analysis_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the analysis method (e.g., regression, clustering) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the analysis method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., data analyst, urban planner) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
